text:
THORACIC ONCOLOGY / CLINICAL TRIAL SITE VISIT NOTE
Oncologist Office Assessment — Phase I/II Investigational EGFR/PI3K Inhibitor Trial (Protocol NCT-XXXXXX)
Cycle 2 Response Assessment

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT / REASON FOR VISIT:
Margaret Chen is a 64-year-old female with stage IV (M1c) EGFR L858R-mutant non-small cell lung cancer, adenocarcinoma, presenting today for her scheduled on-trial clinical assessment following completion of Cycle 2 of investigational therapy on Phase I/II protocol NCT-XXXXXX (investigational EGFR inhibitor + PI3K alpha-selective inhibitor combination). Primary purpose of visit: response assessment review, toxicity evaluation, protocol compliance review, and cycle 3 treatment decision.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS:
Ms. Chen is a 64-year-old woman with a well-characterized history of left upper lobe lung adenocarcinoma, initially diagnosed approximately 2 years ago as Stage IIIB (T3N3M0) disease harboring an EGFR exon 21 L858R activating mutation (allele frequency 38%) with co-occurring TP53 R175H and CDKN2A homozygous deletion on baseline NGS. She achieved a durable partial response on first-line osimertinib (Tagrisso) 80 mg daily, with the left upper lobe primary shrinking from 4.2 cm down to 1.5 cm at nadir over approximately 9 months of therapy. Unfortunately, she progressed at the 14-month mark with both systemic progression (enlarging left upper lobe mass, new hilar adenopathy, new right adrenal nodule) and simultaneous development of three new brain metastases identified on MRI (right frontal 1.4 cm, right parietal 0.8 cm, left cerebellar 0.6 cm). Repeat NGS from the right adrenal biopsy confirmed acquisition of EGFR T790M (allele frequency 31%) as a canonical osimertinib resistance mechanism, as well as a newly acquired PIK3CA E545K mutation (allele frequency 19%), which has been recognized increasingly as a co-resistance driver in EGFR-mutant NSCLC.

Following osimertinib discontinuation, she underwent stereotactic radiosurgery (SRS) via Gamma Knife Perfexion to all three brain metastases (right frontal 20 Gy/1 fraction, right parietal 20 Gy/1 fraction, left cerebellar 18 Gy/1 fraction). She tolerated SRS well with expected transient grade 2 fatigue and grade 1 headache that resolved after a brief dexamethasone taper. She then received four cycles of induction carboplatin (AUC 5) plus pemetrexed (Alimta, 500 mg/m2) every 21 days, complicated by grade 3 neutropenia in cycle 2 requiring a one-week delay and filgrastim support, followed by successful transition to G-CSF prophylaxis in subsequent cycles. She completed induction chemotherapy and transitioned to pemetrexed maintenance therapy, during which she demonstrated only stable disease initially but then systemic progression (left upper lobe mass growth to 4.0 cm, right adrenal increasing to 2.0 cm, new hepatic segment VI lesion of 0.9 cm, new small right pleural effusion) after approximately 3 maintenance cycles.

Given her unique resistance profile — specifically the co-occurrence of EGFR T790M (while noting the absence of EGFR C797S) and PIK3CA E545K — she was enrolled on this Phase I/II investigational trial approximately 8 weeks ago. The trial combines an investigational EGFR inhibitor with activity against T790M/C797S-resistant conformations with a PI3K alpha-selective inhibitor (alpelisib analog), targeting both identified resistance pathways simultaneously. She is currently in the dose expansion cohort and began Cycle 1 Day 1 approximately 8 weeks ago.

Since initiating trial therapy, Ms. Chen reports subjectively feeling somewhat better than she did on her last weeks of pemetrexed maintenance. She describes a modest improvement in her exertional capacity. She denies new cough, hemoptysis, dyspnea at rest, chest pain, or worsening shortness of breath. She reports the mild oral soreness that was noted at cycle 2 has largely resolved. She endorses persistent but manageable nausea, particularly in the mornings, which has been controlled with ondansetron as needed. She denies vomiting. Bowel habits are normal; no diarrhea. She reports skin changes on her face consistent with the mild maculopapular rash noted at cycle 2, which she describes as stable and not bothersome. No new skin changes elsewhere.

Significantly, her blood sugars have improved since the metformin dose was increased following grade 2 hyperglycemia noted in cycle 2. She is monitoring fasting glucose at home and reports readings predominantly in the 130-160 mg/dL range over the past two weeks, improved from the 200+ mg/dL readings earlier in the cycle. She is adhering to the low-carbohydrate dietary counseling from the trial dietitian. She was already on metformin at baseline for her type 2 diabetes mellitus; the PI3K inhibitor component of the trial predictably exacerbated her hyperglycemia, which has been a recognized on-target toxicity of this class.

Neurologically, Ms. Chen remains completely intact. She reports no headaches, no visual changes, no dizziness, no ataxia, no focal weakness or numbness. She is fully independent in activities of daily living. She lives with her husband who accompanies her to visits and is supportive.

She is fully engaged and motivated in trial participation. She has been compliant with all oral medications, fasting glucose logs, and study-required clinic visits.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS:
- Constitutional: Mild fatigue (grade 1), no significant weight loss since last visit. Appetite has been adequate; denies early satiety or significant anorexia. No fevers or chills.
- HEENT: Mild facial rash (stable grade 1, improved from grade 2 noted at cycle 1 onset). Mild residual oral sensitivity, improved from grade 1 mucositis at prior visit. No new oral lesions. No visual disturbances.
- Cardiovascular: No chest pain, palpitations, or peripheral edema. No syncope or presyncope.
- Pulmonary: Mild exertional dyspnea with significant activity (1-2 flights of stairs), unchanged from prior. No dyspnea at rest. No pleuritic chest pain. No hemoptysis. No new cough beyond her chronic mild dry cough.
- Gastrointestinal: Morning nausea, controlled with ondansetron PRN. No vomiting. No diarrhea or constipation. No abdominal pain. No new jaundice.
- Genitourinary: Normal urinary frequency, no dysuria. No hematuria.
- Musculoskeletal: No new bone pain. No joint pain.
- Neurological: No headaches. No focal deficits. No confusion or memory difficulties.
- Endocrine: Fasting glucose improving with metformin dose adjustment and dietary modifications. No polyuria or polydipsia.
- Skin: Mild maculopapular rash on face/upper chest, stable. No blistering, no desquamation, no new sites involved.
- Psychological: No active depression or anxiety per patient report. Coping well. Positive attitude toward trial participation.

---

MEDICATIONS:
1. Investigational EGFR inhibitor (protocol-defined dose, oral) — per trial arm, taken daily with food
2. PI3K alpha-selective inhibitor (alpelisib analog, protocol-defined dose, oral) — per trial arm, taken with food, per protocol schedule
3. Metformin 1000 mg orally twice daily (dose recently increased from 500 mg twice daily for PI3K inhibitor-associated hyperglycemia)
4. Amlodipine 5 mg orally daily (for hypertension)
5. Lisinopril 10 mg orally daily (for hypertension)
6. Folic acid 400 mcg orally daily (continuation from prior pemetrexed protocol; continued per trial protocol guidance)
7. Vitamin B12 1000 mcg IM every 9 weeks (last administered approximately 7 weeks ago — due for repeat next visit)
8. Ondansetron 8 mg orally every 8 hours as needed for nausea
9. Doxycycline 100 mg orally daily (residual prophylactic use for EGFR-class rash, carried forward from initial osimertinib rash management; discussed whether to continue or taper — will reassess)
10. Topical clindamycin gel applied to face as needed for rash
11. Aspirin 81 mg orally daily
12. Pantoprazole 40 mg orally daily

ALLERGIES: No known drug allergies.

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY:
- Stage IV (M1c) NSCLC, adenocarcinoma, EGFR L858R-mutant — primary diagnosis as above
- Hypertension — well-controlled on current regimen
- Type 2 diabetes mellitus — previously managed with metformin 500 mg twice daily; now escalated due to PI3K inhibitor effect
- Former smoker, 25 pack-year history (quit approximately 10 years prior to today)
- Status post stereotactic radiosurgery to three brain metastases (right frontal, right parietal, left cerebellar)
- Status post carboplatin/pemetrexed x4 cycles with pemetrexed maintenance x3 cycles

SOCIAL HISTORY:
Retired accountant. Lives with her husband. Non-smoker (former, quit >8 years prior to original diagnosis). No alcohol use reported. No illicit substance use. College-educated; engaged and articulate in discussing her treatment plan. Has a strong support system.

FAMILY HISTORY:
Mother with breast cancer (diagnosed in her 70s). Father with coronary artery disease. No known family history of lung cancer or other primary lung malignancy. No known germline cancer syndromes in family.

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION:

Vital Signs:
- Blood pressure: 128/76 mmHg
- Heart rate: 76 bpm, regular
- Respiratory rate: 16 breaths/min
- Temperature: 36.8°C
- SpO2: 96% on room air
- Weight: 61.3 kg (stable, up 0.4 kg from last visit)
- Height: 162 cm
- BSA: 1.65 m2 (Mosteller)
- ECOG Performance Status: 1

General: Well-appearing woman, in no acute distress. Alert and oriented x3. Cooperative and engaged.

Skin: Mild maculopapular eruption involving bilateral cheeks, nasal bridge, and upper chest, grade 1, with no open lesions, no pustulation, and no desquamation. Mild post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation on chin from prior grade 2 acneiform rash. No palmar-plantar erythema. Mild residual paronychia left index finger, healing, with no active purulence. Nails otherwise unremarkable.

HEENT: Sclerae anicteric. Pupils equal, round, reactive to light. Oral mucosa moist with minimal residual erythema of buccal mucosa, grade 1 at most, no ulceration. No cervical or supraclavicular lymphadenopathy palpated (notably, prior left supraclavicular adenopathy has not been clinically evident since early response to osimertinib).

Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. No murmurs, rubs, or gallops. No jugular venous distension. No lower extremity edema.

Pulmonary: Mildly decreased breath sounds over the left upper lobe, unchanged from prior assessments. No dullness to percussion over right or left lower lung fields (prior small right-sided pleural effusion appears to have resolved clinically, consistent with imaging). No wheeze, no rhonchi. No stridor.

Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No organomegaly appreciated. Bowel sounds normoactive. No palpable masses.

Neurological: Alert and oriented to person, place, time. Cranial nerves II-XII grossly intact. Motor strength 5/5 in all extremities. Gait steady. Finger-nose-finger testing intact bilaterally. No pronator drift. Reflexes symmetric and normal.

Musculoskeletal: No focal bony tenderness. Full range of motion of extremities.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS (obtained prior to today's visit):

Complete Blood Count:
- WBC: 5.2 x 10^9/L (reference 4.0-11.0) — within normal limits
- ANC: 3.1 x 10^9/L — adequate, no neutropenia
- Hemoglobin: 11.4 g/dL (reference 12.0-16.0) — mild anemia, stable/improved from nadir during chemotherapy
- Platelets: 198 x 10^9/L — within normal limits

Comprehensive Metabolic Panel:
- Sodium: 138 mEq/L
- Potassium: 4.0 mEq/L
- Creatinine: 0.82 mg/dL (eGFR >60 mL/min/1.73m2)
- Total bilirubin: 0.7 mg/dL
- AST: 28 U/L
- ALT: 32 U/L
- Alkaline phosphatase: 74 U/L
- Albumin: 3.8 g/dL

Fasting Glucose: 148 mg/dL — improved from 210 mg/dL at cycle 2 trough; consistent with grade 1 hyperglycemia now (down from grade 2)

HbA1c (most recent, from 4 weeks ago): 7.2% — modestly elevated, but reflecting pre-trial diabetes management; will recheck at next assessment.

ECG (performed today):
- Normal sinus rhythm, rate 76 bpm
- QTc: 438 ms (Fridericia correction) — within acceptable range per trial protocol. No significant change from prior QTc measurements (432 ms at 3-month osimertinib assessment, 438 ms at last trial visit). Serial monitoring showing no trend toward QTc prolongation. No arrhythmia.

---

IMAGING RESULTS:

CT Chest/Abdomen/Pelvis with Contrast (obtained approximately 3-4 days prior to today's visit, reviewed in detail):
Compared to pre-trial baseline (left upper lobe mass 4.0 cm, right adrenal nodule 2.0 cm, hepatic segment VI lesion 0.9 cm, right pleural effusion small):
- Left upper lobe mass: decreased from 4.0 cm to 3.0 cm — a meaningful reduction, with the mass appearing less conspicuous and slightly less spiculated at its margins compared to pre-trial imaging. The chest wall and major airways remain uninvolved.
- Right adrenal nodule: stable at 2.0 cm — no meaningful change.
- Hepatic segment VI lesion: decreased from 0.9 cm to 0.6 cm — a reassuring reduction, though the lesion remains present.
- Right pleural effusion: near-complete resolution — only a trace if any at this assessment; this is a welcome change from the small-moderate effusion noted at second progression.
- No new pulmonary nodules. No new pleural disease. No new mediastinal or hilar adenopathy beyond what is known. No new hepatic lesions.
- Bony structures: No lytic or blastic lesions to suggest new skeletal metastases.

Overall impression: The CT findings are encouraging. There has been meaningful reduction in the primary lung mass and the liver metastasis, with resolution of the pleural effusion, and stability of the adrenal nodule. This is consistent with a partial response to the investigational therapy, representing the best response we have seen since the osimertinib era. Total target lesion burden has decreased by approximately 25% from the pre-trial baseline sum of diameters.

(MRI brain not obtained this visit per protocol; most recent MRI brain obtained at pre-trial enrollment showed stable post-treatment changes at all three SRS sites with no new intracranial metastases. Next brain MRI planned per protocol.)

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN:

Impression: Ms. Chen is a 64-year-old woman with stage IV EGFR L858R-mutant NSCLC, adenocarcinoma, with acquired T790M/PIK3CA E545K co-resistance, currently on Cycle 2 of Phase I/II investigational EGFR/PI3K dual inhibitor combination therapy. Today's visit confirms an early partial response to this investigational regimen, with her left upper lobe primary and hepatic metastasis both showing meaningful shrinkage and her right pleural effusion resolving. The right adrenal nodule is stable. She is performing at ECOG PS 1 and tolerating therapy quite well overall.

1. Investigational Therapy — Continuing:
   The early imaging response is encouraging and consistent with biologic rationale — her tumor harbors both EGFR T790M (primary osimertinib resistance driver) and PIK3CA E545K (co-resistance/bypass signaling driver), and targeting both simultaneously via this combination appears to be producing clinical benefit. We reviewed the CT findings together with the patient and her husband in detail. She remains enthusiastic about participation. She will proceed to Cycle 3 per protocol today. Trial pharmacy has confirmed drug supply. Protocol-specified labs are completed and within acceptable parameters to proceed. Informed consent reaffirmed.

2. Hyperglycemia (PI3K Inhibitor-associated) — Improving, Grade 1:
   Fasting glucose today 148 mg/dL, improved from 210 mg/dL (grade 2) at the cycle 2 decision point. Metformin escalation to 1000 mg twice daily and dietary modification have been effective. Will continue current metformin dose. Weekly fasting glucose home monitoring ongoing. HbA1c to be rechecked at next visit (cycle 3 assessment). Endocrinology co-management was offered but patient and I agree to continue managing jointly through the trial team at this time given good response to escalation. Will set a threshold of grade 3 hyperglycemia (fasting glucose >250 mg/dL or symptomatic) for mandatory endocrine referral and/or dose reduction per protocol. No dose reduction required at this time.

3. Mucositis — Resolved:
   Grade 1 oral mucositis noted at cycle 2 has largely resolved by patient report, with only minimal residual buccal erythema on exam. No ulceration. Counseled on continued good oral hygiene. Recommend continued use of alcohol-free rinses. If mucositis recurs or worsens, we will escalate to trial-approved supportive care measures. No dose modification required at this time.

4. Maculopapular Rash — Stable Grade 1, No Dose Modification Required:
   The mild facial/upper chest rash has been managed effectively with topical clindamycin and oral doxycycline (carried forward from prior EGFR-associated rash management). I discussed with Ms. Chen whether to taper the doxycycline given improvement; we agreed to continue at 100 mg daily for now given stability and good tolerability. Topical clindamycin continued PRN. Reviewed grade criteria; no dose modification warranted. Dermatology referral available if it progresses.

5. Anemia:
   Hemoglobin 11.4 g/dL, mild, and stable/slightly improved from chemotherapy nadir. Asymptomatic. No transfusion indicated. Will continue to monitor. Iron stores and B12 levels will be checked at next cycle assessment given ongoing supplementation.

6. Vitamin B12 Injection:
   Due for repeat vitamin B12 1000 mcg IM at next visit (approximately 2 weeks from today per schedule). Nursing staff instructed to flag for administration at Cycle 3 visit.

7. QTc Monitoring:
   QTc 438 ms today — within acceptable protocol range. Serial monitoring showing no trend. No antiarrhythmic drug interactions noted. Will continue per-protocol ECG monitoring (every cycle). If QTc exceeds 470 ms in women, will follow dose-modification guidelines per trial protocol.

8. Hypertension:
   Blood pressure 128/76 today — well-controlled on current regimen (amlodipine + lisinopril). No change to antihypertensive management.

9. Brain Metastases — Stable (Post-SRS):
   Ms. Chen remains neurologically intact. Most recent brain MRI (obtained at pre-trial enrollment) showed stable post-treatment changes at all three SRS sites, with no new intracranial disease and one lesion (right frontal) showing central necrosis consistent with radiation necrosis/treatment effect rather than progression. Per protocol, next brain MRI is planned following Cycle 4 completion. She continues to be counseled on neurologic symptoms to report urgently (new headache, visual changes, focal weakness, ataxia, seizures).

10. Goals of Care and Prognosis:
    I spent considerable time speaking with Ms. Chen and her husband today about her overall trajectory, what this early response means, and what to expect going forward. I emphasized that while the early response is genuinely encouraging and gives us cautious optimism, this is an investigational therapy and durability of response remains to be established. I reinforced the palliative intent of current therapy. She remains fully informed of her diagnosis and prognosis and continues to prioritize quality of life and meaningful time with her family. She has an established relationship with palliative care which we encourage her to maintain. Advance care directive is documented and current.

11. Follow-up:
    Return in approximately 3 weeks for Cycle 3 Day 1 assessment, labs, ECG, and protocol-mandated safety assessments. Vitamin B12 injection due at that visit. Next CT response assessment planned after Cycle 4 per protocol. Brain MRI also planned after Cycle 4.

---

Electronic signature: [Attending Thoracic Oncologist / Principal Investigator, Clinical Trials Office]
Time spent: 45 minutes total, greater than 50% spent in counseling and care coordination.

---
